<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Nervous stick figure -->
  <!-- Head -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="190" r="70" fill="#ffe0c7" stroke="#222" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Eyebrows (tense) -->
  <path d="M205 165 Q224 148 245 165" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M267 165 Q287 148 307 165" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Eyes (nervous/alert) -->
  <path d="M220 205 Q236 190 252 205" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M260 205 Q276 190 292 205" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <circle cx="238" cy="210" r="6" fill="#222"/>
  <circle cx="280" cy="210" r="6" fill="#222"/>

  <!-- Mouth (slight worry) -->
  <path d="M238 245 Q256 260 274 245" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Sweat drops -->
  <path d="M235 135 C220 150 220 175 235 185 C250 175 250 150 235 135 Z" fill="#4aa3ff" opacity="0.95" stroke="#1f6fb8" stroke-width="3"/>
  <path d="M275 140 C262 154 262 177 275 186 C288 177 288 154 275 140 Z" fill="#4aa3ff" opacity="0.95" stroke="#1f6fb8" stroke-width="3"/>
  <path d="M255 120 C244 132 244 152 255 160 C266 152 266 132 255 120 Z" fill="#4aa3ff" opacity="0.95" stroke="#1f6fb8" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Body -->
  <path d="M256 260 C240 290 235 320 240 360 C245 402 267 402 272 360 C277 320 272 290 256 260 Z" fill="#ffd7b8" stroke="#222" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Arms (trembling) -->
  <path d="M200 290 C175 315 170 340 185 360" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M312 290 C337 315 342 340 327 360" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Hands -->
  <circle cx="180" cy="362" r="16" fill="#ffd7b8" stroke="#222" stroke-width="4"/>
  <circle cx="332" cy="362" r="16" fill="#ffd7b8" stroke="#222" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Legs -->
  <path d="M240 360 C220 395 215 420 220 455" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M272 360 C292 395 297 420 292 455" fill="none" stroke="#222" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Shaky lines around (nervousness) -->
  <g stroke="#6b5cff" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.85">
    <path d="M120 250 C140 230 155 235 165 260" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M92 290 C120 275 140 290 146 320" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M132 360 C150 330 180 330 195 360" fill="none"/>

    <path d="M392 250 C372 230 357 235 347 260" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M420 290 C392 275 372 290 366 320" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M380 360 C362 330 332 330 317 360" fill="none"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Subtle ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="475" rx="120" ry="18" fill="#000" opacity="0.08"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a visual representation of "nervous"**

I'm planning to illustrate the meaning of "nervous" without any text. I want to use visuals like shaking hands, sweat, or exclamation marks, but no letters. I can depict a person with a tense posture, sweat drops, and wavy lines to show their anxiety. A simple stick figure with trembling lines and sweat drops will work. I’ll make sure to keep it to a 512 canvas, focusing on a worried expression and zigzag lines to emphasize nervousness. I'll return it as an SVG.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
